name: System Homomorphisms
description: Definition of system homomorphisms: Z1 is a homomorphic image of Z2 with respect to state, input, and output homomorphisms satisfying next-state and readout consistency.

elements.text: Z1 is a homomorphic image of Z2 with respect to HS, HI, HO if and only if (i) HS ∈ FNS(SZ2, ONTO, SZ1), (ii) HI ∈ FNS(IZ2, ONTO, IZ1), (iii) HO ∈ FNS(OZ2, ONTO, OZ1), (iv) HS(NZ2(x,p)) = NZ1(HS(x), HI(p)) for all x ∈ SZ2 and p ∈ IZ2, and (v) HO(RZ2(x)) = RZ1(HS(x)) for all x ∈ SZ2.
Condition (iv) is the next-state consistency requirement.
Condition (v) is the readout consistency requirement.
If Z1 is a homomorphic image of Z2, then Z1 is a consistent simplification of Z2 and Z2 is a consistent elaboration of Z1.
If Z1 is a homomorphic image of Z2, then Z2 has the functional capability of Z1.
